name: Risotto Primavera
recipeIngredient: 1 pound pencil-thin asparagus, trimmed, cut, diagonally (about 1 1/2 inch lengths)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, heat the broth to a simmer and keep it hot over very low heat.
2. In a large heavy skillet, combine 2 tablespoons of butter and olive oil. Cook onion and garlic over medium heat for about 5 minutes or until crisp-tender.
3. Add rice and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes. Stir in wine and lemon zest, cooking until the wine is absorbed.
4. Raise the heat to medium-high. Stir in half a cup of hot broth; cook, stirring, until most of the liquid evaporates (leaves a track on the bottom of the pan with no surrounding liquid).
5. Cook for another 10 minutes, adding broth in half-cup increments as it evaporates. Continue stirring.
6. Add asparagus, bell pepper, squash, and black pepper; cook for an additional 12 to 15 minutes, adding hot broth in half-cups until the rice is firm yet tender, a creamy sauce forms, and the vegetables are tender.
7. Remove from heat and stir in remaining butter, parsley, and Parmesan cheese. Serve immediately. Makes 8 servings.
